name: resizable-panels
title: Resizable Panels
description: A split-pane layout with draggable, keyboard-operable separators. Each ResizableHandle is a real role=separator implementing the WAI-ARIA Window Splitter pattern with aria-orientation, aria-valuenow/min/max, and aria-controls. Arrow keys resize by a step (mirrored in RTL), Home/End jump to min/max, and Enter/Space optionally collapse. Below a breakpoint the panels stack and the separators go inert so the layout reflows (WCAG 1.4.10). Self-contained (no third-party resizable dependency).
useWhen: A list + detail layout where the reader controls how much space each pane gets
A code/preview or content/inspector split that should remember its sizes
Any dashboard region where users benefit from resizing panes to their content
avoidWhen: Fixed layouts that never need user resizing (use plain flex/grid)
Collapsible disclosure of a single region (use a Collapsible or Drawer)
Small-screen-only layouts where panes should always stack (skip the separators)

keyboard: Tab moves focus to each separator; Shift+Tab moves away
Arrow keys resize by the step, mirrored under dir=rtl for horizontal groups
Home moves the resized panel to its minimum; End to its maximum
Enter or Space toggles a collapse to the minimum when the handle is collapsible
Double-click resets both panels to their default sizes
When the group stacks below the breakpoint the separators leave the tab order
accessibility.targetSize: 44px project default (the thin separator line carries an overlapping 44px hit area)
notes: Each separator exposes role=separator with aria-orientation, aria-valuenow/min/max (the resized panel's size %), aria-valuetext, and aria-controls referencing the panel it resizes.

Non-color redundancy: the separator keeps a visible border/grip in every state and conveys size via aria-valuetext and panel position, so it survives Windows High Contrast / forced-colors mode where the accent fill is overridden.
Reflow (WCAG 1.4.10): below stackAt the panels stack into one column and the separators are removed from the DOM a11y tree and tab order so content reflows for small screens and 400% zoom.
RTL: horizontal resize direction inverts under dir=rtl for both pointer drag and Arrow keys; logical properties and mirrored transforms keep the visuals correct.
Grip color/position transitions are gated behind motion-safe (prefers-reduced-motion respected); resizing itself is instantaneous.
